title: Prove cumulative bit encoding is exhaustively enforced

artifacts.description: Add explicit implication constraints: bit4=>bit3, bit4=>bit2, bit3=>bit2. Currently enforced in Delegation but must also be enforced at credential-minting time in AgentPolicy so an agent cannot mint a structurally invalid bitmask.

Exhaustive witness-generation tests over all 256 possible 8-bit permissionBitmask values. For each satisfying assignment assert implication rules hold; for each violating assignment assert witness generation fails (invalid constraint).

Property tests proving: (1) if delegatorScope is valid (passes cumulative rules) and delegateeScope is a bitwise subset of delegatorScope, then delegateeScope is also valid. (2) If delegateeScope violates implication rules independently, the Delegation circuit rejects it even when it is a subset of delegatorScope.
Add three new named properties: CUMUL-1 (bit4 implies bits 2+3), CUMUL-2 (bit3 implies bit2), SUBSET-CLOSURE (subset of valid scope is valid). Reference the test files that witness each property.
Record that cumulative-bit enforcement must be duplicated in every circuit that accepts a permissionBitmask input, not only in Delegation.

steps: Read AgentPolicy.circom in full; locate where permissionBitmask is decomposed into bits and identify which implication constraints (if any) are already present.
Read Delegation.circom to extract the exact gate pattern used for bit4=>bit3+bit2 and bit3=>bit2 implications (source of truth for constraint style).

If AgentPolicy.circom is missing any of the three implication constraints, add them using the same gate pattern; keep constraint count delta minimal (each implication is one quadratic constraint via 1 - bit_i * (1 - bit_j) == 0 or equivalent).

Write circuits/test/agentPolicy.cumulative.test.js: loop all 256 values of permissionBitmask; for each, compute expected validity from the three rules, then call circom_tester.calculateWitness() and assert it either succeeds or throws.

Write circuits/test/delegation.composition.test.js: (a) property SUBSET-CLOSURE — pick 20 random valid delegatorScopes, derive all bitwise subsets, confirm each subset is accepted by Delegation circuit; (b) anti-property — take a structurally invalid delegateeScope that is still a bitwise subset of some delegatorScope and confirm Delegation rejects it.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to verify all new tests pass without full proof generation.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with CUMUL-1, CUMUL-2, SUBSET-CLOSURE property statements and cross-link the test file + line numbers.
Run npm run compile:circuits to confirm no constraint count regressions beyond the expected delta (≤3 new constraints in AgentPolicy).
Append a one-line lesson to tasks/lessons.md documenting the enforcement-duplication requirement.
Commit with DCO sign-off: git commit -s -m 'feat(circuits): enforce cumulative-bit implications in AgentPolicy + property tests'
dependencies: pot16.ptau (only needed for slow proof path, not required for this witness-only property suite)
